provisions.title: Immediate Outcome 1: ML/TF Risk Understanding
provisions.text: The country understands its ML/TF risks. This outcome relates to FATF Recommendations 1 and 2 and covers: whether the country has identified, assessed and understood its ML/TF risks; whether the results of risk assessment are used to inform policy, allocate resources, and prioritise AML/CFT activities; and whether exemptions and enhanced measures are applied consistently with the risk assessment findings. A country should have a comprehensive, periodically updated national risk assessment (NRA).

provisions.title: Immediate Outcome 2: International Cooperation
provisions.text: International cooperation delivers appropriate information, financial intelligence, and evidence, and facilitates action against criminals and their assets. This covers: whether competent authorities provide constructive and timely mutual legal assistance and extradition; whether competent authorities seek mutual legal assistance and other forms of international cooperation for ML/TF-related matters; and whether appropriate international cooperation mechanisms are in place.

provisions.title: Immediate Outcome 3: Supervision
provisions.text: Supervisors appropriately supervise, monitor, and regulate financial institutions and DNFBPs for compliance with AML/CFT requirements commensurate with their risks. This covers: whether licensing/registration prevents criminals from holding controlling interests; whether supervisors apply a risk-based approach to supervision; whether remedial actions and sanctions are applied effectively; and whether supervisors promote understanding of AML/CFT obligations.

provisions.title: Immediate Outcome 4: Preventive Measures
provisions.text: Financial institutions and DNFBPs adequately apply AML/CFT preventive measures commensurate with their risks and report suspicious transactions. This covers: whether institutions apply adequate CDD measures; whether institutions apply enhanced measures for higher-risk situations; whether suspicious transactions are reported; whether institutions implement internal controls, compliance management, and training; and whether financial groups implement group-wide programmes.

provisions.title: Immediate Outcome 5: Legal Persons and Arrangements
provisions.text: Legal persons and arrangements are prevented from misuse for ML/TF, and information on their beneficial ownership is available to competent authorities without impediments. This covers: whether adequate, accurate and timely beneficial ownership information is available; whether competent authorities have timely access; whether measures prevent misuse of nominee shareholders and directors; and whether sanctions are applied for non-compliance with beneficial ownership requirements.

provisions.title: Immediate Outcome 6: Financial Intelligence
provisions.text: Financial intelligence and all other relevant information are appropriately used by competent authorities for ML/TF investigations. This covers: whether the FIU produces financial intelligence and disseminates it to competent authorities; whether the FIU and other competent authorities use financial intelligence to develop evidence and trace criminal proceeds; and whether financial intelligence contributes to identifying ML/TF threats, vulnerabilities, and networks.

provisions.title: Immediate Outcome 7: ML Investigation and Prosecution
provisions.text: ML offences and activities are investigated and offenders are prosecuted and subject to effective, proportionate and dissuasive sanctions. This covers: whether ML is identified and investigated consistently with the country's risk profile; whether different types of ML cases are pursued (stand-alone, third-party, self-laundering, foreign predicate); whether effective sanctions are applied; and whether alternative criminal justice measures are used where appropriate.

provisions.title: Immediate Outcome 8: Confiscation
provisions.text: Proceeds and instrumentalities of crime are confiscated. This covers: whether confiscation is pursued as a policy objective; whether the confiscation of proceeds, instrumentalities and property of equivalent value is achieved; whether confiscation results are consistent with the country's ML/TF risk profile; and whether confiscated assets are managed and disposed of appropriately. Countries should maintain statistics on confiscation outcomes.

provisions.title: Immediate Outcome 9: TF Investigation and Prosecution
provisions.text: TF offences and activities are investigated and persons who finance terrorism are prosecuted and subject to effective, proportionate and dissuasive sanctions. This covers: whether TF is investigated and prosecuted consistently with the country's risk profile; whether TF investigations identify and address links between terrorism and its financing; and whether effective sanctions are applied to persons convicted of TF offences.

provisions.title: Immediate Outcome 10: TF Preventive Measures and Financial Sanctions
provisions.text: Terrorists, terrorist organisations and terrorist financiers are prevented from raising, moving, and using funds, and from abusing the NPO sector. This covers: whether targeted financial sanctions related to terrorism and TF are implemented without delay; whether persons and entities designated under UNSC resolutions are deprived of their assets; whether NPOs are protected from TF abuse; and whether the country has measures in place to identify and disrupt cross-border movement of terrorist funds.

provisions.title: Immediate Outcome 11: PF Financial Sanctions
provisions.text: Persons and entities involved in the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction are prevented from raising, moving, and using funds, consistent with the relevant UNSCRs. This covers: whether targeted financial sanctions related to proliferation financing (PF) are implemented promptly; whether designated persons and entities are identified and their assets frozen; and whether financial institutions and DNFBPs screen against PF designation lists.

provisions.title: FATF Technical Compliance Rating Scale
provisions.text: Technical compliance with each FATF Recommendation is assessed on a four-level rating scale: Compliant (C) means the country fully meets the requirements; Largely Compliant (LC) means only minor shortcomings exist; Partially Compliant (PC) means moderate shortcomings exist; and Non-Compliant (NC) means major shortcomings exist. The assessment is based on whether the country's legal and institutional framework meets the essential criteria for each Recommendation.

provisions.title: FATF Effectiveness Rating Scale
provisions.text: Effectiveness for each Immediate Outcome is assessed on a four-level rating scale: High level of effectiveness (HE) means the IO is achieved to a very large extent with only minor improvements needed; Substantial level of effectiveness (SE) means the IO is achieved to a large extent with moderate improvements needed; Moderate level of effectiveness (ME) means the IO is achieved to some extent with major improvements needed; and Low level of effectiveness (LE) means the IO is not achieved or achieved to a negligible extent with fundamental improvements needed.

provisions.title: Risk-Based Approach Guidance for Banks
provisions.text: FATF guidance on the risk-based approach for the banking sector covers: identification of ML/TF risks (customer risk, country/geographic risk, product/service risk, delivery channel risk); risk assessment methodology (inherent risk, quality of controls, residual risk); risk mitigation (simplified CDD for lower risk, enhanced CDD for higher risk); and ongoing monitoring systems (transaction monitoring parameters, alert management, model validation). Banks should document their risk assessment methodology and update it regularly.

provisions.title: Risk-Based Approach for Virtual Asset Service Providers
provisions.text: FATF guidance on the risk-based approach for VASPs covers: VASP registration and licensing requirements; identification and mitigation of VA-specific ML/TF risks (pseudonymity, speed, cross-border nature, P2P transfers); implementation of the travel rule for VA transfers (originator and beneficiary information); blockchain analytics and transaction monitoring; and supervision of VASPs by competent authorities. Countries that prohibit VA activities should have mechanisms to identify and sanction unlicensed VASPs operating in their jurisdiction.

provisions.title: Proliferation Financing Risk Assessment Guidance
provisions.text: FATF guidance on proliferation financing (PF) risk assessment requires countries to identify, assess and understand their PF risks. The risk assessment should cover: the PF threat (types of WMD programmes, procurement networks, financing channels); PF vulnerability (financial products and services that could be exploited, geographic and sectoral exposure); the effectiveness of existing counter-PF measures; and the adequacy of the legal and institutional framework for implementing UNSCRs. Countries should apply a risk-based approach to allocating counter-PF resources.

provisions.title: FATF Guidance on Beneficial Ownership of Legal Persons
provisions.text: FATF guidance on beneficial ownership transparency for legal persons covers: the multi-pronged approach to BO transparency (central registers, company-held information, existing information held by obliged entities); requirements for BO registers (public access where possible, adequate staffing and resources, quality assurance processes); verification mechanisms (discrepancy reporting, cross-referencing with other data sources); access by competent authorities (timely, direct access, no restrictions); and sanctions for non-compliance (effective, proportionate, dissuasive). The guidance was updated in 2023 to align with the revised Recommendation 24.

provisions.title: Targeted Financial Sanctions: UN Designations Implementation
provisions.text: Countries should implement targeted financial sanctions (TFS) to comply with United Nations Security Council Resolutions relating to terrorism and proliferation. This requires: a legal framework enabling freezing of assets without delay; procedures for identifying persons and entities designated by the UN or domestically; communication of designations to the financial sector; mechanisms for unfreezing assets of de-listed persons; and procedures for authorising access to frozen funds for basic expenses. Implementation should be effective within 24 hours of a UN designation.

provisions.title: Democratic People's Republic of Korea - High Risk
provisions.text: The DPRK (North Korea) is identified by the FATF as a high-risk jurisdiction subject to a call for action. The FATF calls on its members and urges all jurisdictions to apply counter-measures to protect the international financial system from the ongoing and substantial ML/TF/PF risks emanating from the DPRK. Jurisdictions should apply enhanced due diligence measures proportionate to the risks and should terminate correspondent relationships with DPRK banks.

provisions.title: Iran - High Risk
provisions.text: Iran is identified by the FATF as a high-risk jurisdiction subject to a call for action. Despite Iran's high-level political commitment to address its strategic AML/CFT deficiencies, Iran has not completed its action plan regarding the strategic deficiencies identified by the FATF. The FATF calls on its members and all jurisdictions to apply effective counter-measures, including enhanced due diligence and restrictions on financial transactions with Iran.

provisions.title: Myanmar - High Risk
provisions.text: Myanmar is identified by the FATF as a high-risk jurisdiction subject to a call for action. The FATF is concerned by the lack of progress in addressing AML/CFT deficiencies, particularly since the February 2021 military takeover. The FATF calls on its members and all jurisdictions to apply enhanced due diligence measures proportionate to the risks arising from Myanmar.

fatf_country_status.status: High-Risk Jurisdiction subject to a Call for Action
fatf_country_status.notes: DPRK has strategic AML/CFT/PF deficiencies. Counter-measures required.

fatf_country_status.status: High-Risk Jurisdiction subject to a Call for Action
fatf_country_status.notes: Iran has not completed its AML/CFT action plan. Counter-measures required.

fatf_country_status.status: High-Risk Jurisdiction subject to a Call for Action
fatf_country_status.notes: Lack of progress since February 2021 military takeover.

mutual_evaluations.key_findings: Strong enforcement; beneficial ownership transparency gap; VASP supervision developing; inconsistent state-level supervision of DNFBPs.
Strong supervision and enforcement; gaps in trust and company service provider supervision; ongoing improvements to Companies House beneficial ownership verification.
Improvements in FIU effectiveness since restructuring; strengthened beneficial ownership register; gaps in DNFBP supervision; need for greater international cooperation in complex ML cases.
Strong AML/CFT framework; effective FIU; risk-based supervision; need to enhance beneficial ownership transparency; challenges with cross-border trade-based ML.
